description: Query Kubernetes resources and read pod logs. Secrets, service accounts, RBAC, and CSR objects are blocked. Writes are not supported.

tools.name: list_pods
tools.description: List pods and their status in a namespace.
tools.returns: Tabular list of pod names and phases.
tools.whenToUse: Use to get a quick overview of pods in a namespace.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when you need detailed pod specs or logs.

tools.name: get_pod_logs
tools.description: Get recent logs from a specific pod.

parameters.name: tail_lines
parameters.description: Number of log lines to retrieve (max 200).

tools.whenToUse: Use for troubleshooting, error investigation, or checking application output.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for pods in namespaces outside the allowlist.

tools.name: kubectl_get
tools.description: Get a summary of Kubernetes resources of a given type.

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: Plural resource type, e.g. pods, services, deployments, nodes.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Namespace for namespaced resources. Ignored for cluster-scoped resources.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional specific resource name. If omitted, lists all resources of the type.

tools.returns: Resource summary or a single resource detail.
tools.whenToUse: Use to inspect deployments, services, nodes, events, or other safe resource types.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for secrets, serviceaccounts, roles, rolebindings, clusterroles, clusterrolebindings, tokenreviews, or certificatesigningrequests.

tools.name: kubectl_describe
tools.description: Describe a Kubernetes resource in detail.
tools.whenToUse: Use when you need events, conditions, or full metadata for debugging.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for blocked resource types or resources outside allowed namespaces.
